rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible ports and features at their respective scope levels.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use valid AADL identifiers and types present in the model.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and avoid invalid scalar type names.
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type contracts.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents or ports in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax requires const, eq, and guraantee declarations to use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Use only package-qualified identifiers that are explicitly imported or defined in the model.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or assignments.
Do not assume numeric or Boolean encodings for event ports without explicit model support.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Model validator diagnostics and repair actions
